key_insight: A two-stage evolutionary hyperparameter search can make PINN training more reliable by using cheap screening runs first and full training only on the best candidates.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a practical way to improve PDE solution accuracy under limited compute, especially when PINN training is unstable and highly sensitive to hyperparameters.
method: Stage 1: run truncated, low-fidelity PINN trainings to evaluate many hyperparameter configurations quickly.
Use an evolutionary algorithm as the outer-loop search over mixed architectural and optimization hyperparameters.
Stage 2: fully train only the top-ranked candidates with standard gradient-based optimizers to refine final performance.
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated on Advection, Klein-Gordon, and Helmholtz problems and consistently reduced mean error versus standard training within fixed computational budgets.
watch_item: The method still depends on the quality of the low-fidelity proxy, so truncated training may mis-rank candidates if early loss behavior does not reflect final PDE accuracy.
paper_title: Evolutionary Two-Stage Hyperparameter Optimization Strategies for Physics-Informed Neural Networks
